items.title: Komatsu expects profits to tumble 16% on Iran war, US tariffs
Russia claims its Africa Corps group prevented coup in Mali after rebels seize towns
items.description: Kremlin-controlled paramilitaries also alleged it inflicted ‘irreplaceable losses’ on insurgents avoiding civilian casualtiesRussia’s defence ministry has claimed its Africa Corps – the successor to the former Wagner mercenary group – had prevented a coup in Mali over the weekend, avoiding mass civilian casualties and …

items.title: Mali capital 'not under threat, but long term prospects do not look good', analyst says
items.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Dr Nathaniel Powell, West Africa Analyst at Oxford Analytica and Research Associate at Lancaster University's Centre for War and Diplomacy, explains that the jihadist group operating in Mali, JNIM, "does not appear to have the capability to seize and hold an urban area for any …

items.title: Israel says no proof Russian grain shipment ‘stolen,’ as Ukraine threatens sanctions
items.description: FM Sa'ar again accuses Zelensky of 'Twitter diplomacy,' after Ukrainian president says Israel 'cannot be unaware' cargo at Haifa port came from Russian-occupied territory The post Israel says no proof Russian grain shipment ‘stolen,’ as Ukraine threatens sanctions appeared first on The Times of Israel.

items.title: IS says it was behind deadly Nigeria attack on football pitch
items.description: Authorities say the militants raided a football pitch where people had been gathering and opened fire at random.

items.title: L'Egitto conferma la condanna di un'italiana per adulterio
items.description: Nessy Guerra originaria di Sanremo e mamma di una bimba di tre anni

items.title: France's awkward stance over deepening crisis in Mali
items.description: Paris has only issued a brief statement since last weekend's jihadists offensive in the country, where the ruling junta, now allied with Russia, is increasingly threatened.

items.title: Kremlin Calls for Stability in Mali as Rebels Seize Kidal and Target Capital
items.description: Moscow said it did not know the current status or whereabouts of Assimi Goïta, the leader of Mali’s military junta.

items.title: North Korea Is Honoring Some Of Its Soldiers Killed In The Ukraine War. But What About The Rest?
items.description: Why a sprawling war memorial that was opened on April 26 commemorates only a fraction of the estimated thousands of troops Pyongyang lost in Russia.

items.title: ‘War crime’: Afghan-Pakistan truce under strain after university strike
items.description: Civilian casualties in Kunar spur tensions as Pakistan denies role, casting shadow over ceasefire and peace talks.

items.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Gaza in focus
items.description: The Security Council turns again to the Middle East crisis in New York on Tuesday, with scheduled debate expected to focus on the fragile Gaza ceasefire, a worsening humanitarian crisis there and efforts to advance a US-backed peace plan. UN Assistant Secretary-General for political affairs Khaled Khiari is set to brie…

items.title: Deadlock over Iran's nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz cripples peace efforts
items.description: Two months after the U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran started the war, peace talks are on hold, with control of the Strait of Hormuz and the future of Iran's nuclear program as the two main points of contention.

items.title: Mali junta destabilized by 'unprecedented' insurgency
items.description: After extremist attacks killed Mali's defense minister and hit several cities, the unrest now poses a serious challenge to the ruling junta, analysts say.

items.title: Two Dead in Kramatorsk as Russian Shelling Hits Residential Area
items.description: A woman and a man were killed while standing on the street during a morning strike on the Donetsk region city, which also damaged eight homes and wounded a 50-year-old civilian.

items.title: Trump cancels envoys' Pakistan trip, in blow to hopes for Iran war breakthrough
items.description: Peace talks between the U.S. and Iran were scheduled to continue Saturday in Pakistan, but they fell apart after President Donald Trump announced that he would not be sending U.S. envoys to take part in negotiations.
